number."}},"nextOutputIndex()":{"returns":{"_0":"The index of the next output to be proposed."}},"proposeL2Output(bytes32,bytes32,uint256,uint256,bytes,address)":{"details":"Modified the function signature to exclude the `_l1BlockHash` parameter, as it's redundant      for OP Succinct given the `_l1BlockNumber` parameter.Security Note: This contract uses `tx.origin` for proposer permission control due to usage of this contract      in the OPSuccinctDisputeGame, created via DisputeGameFactory using the Clone With Immutable Arguments (CWIA) pattern.      In this setup:      - `msg.sender` is the newly created game contract, not an approved proposer.      - `tx.origin` identifies the actual user initiating the transaction.      While `tx.origin` can be vulnerable in general, it is safe here because:      - Only trusted proposers/relayers call this contract.      - Proposers are expected to interact solely with trusted contracts.      As long as proposers avoid untrusted contracts, `tx.origin` is as secure as `msg.sender` in this context.","params":{"_configName":"The name of the OP Succinct configuration to use.","_l1BlockNumber":"The block number with the specified block hash.","_l2BlockNumber":"The L2 block number that resulted in _outputRoot.","_outputRoot":"The L2 output of the checkpoint block.","_proof":"The aggregation proof that proves the transition from the latest L2 output to the new L2 output.","_proverAddress":"The address of the prover that submitted the proof. Note: proverAddress is not required to be the tx.origin as there is no reason to front-run the prover in the full validity setting."}},"proposeL2Output(bytes32,uint256,bytes32,uint256)":{"details":"This function is sourced from the original L2OutputOracle contract. The only modification is that the proposer address must be in the approvedProposers mapping, or permissionless proposing is enabled.This function is not compatible with the `OPSuccinctDisputeGame` contract as it uses `msg.sender` for proposer permission control.      See `whenNotOptimistic` implementation of `proposeL2Output` for more details.      If the functionality for optimistic mode is needed in the `OPSuccinctDisputeGame` contract, use mock mode instead.","params":{"_l1BlockHash":"A block hash which must be included in the current chain.","_l1BlockNumber":"The block number with the specified block hash.","_l2BlockNumber":"The L2 block number that resulted in _outputRoot.","_outputRoot":"The L2 output of the checkpoint block."}},"removeProposer(address)":{"params":{"_proposer":"The proposer address to remove."}},"setDisputeGameFactory(address)":{"params":{"_disputeGameFactory":"The dispute game factory address."}},"transferOwnership(address)":{"params":{"_owner":"The new owner address."}},"updateSubmissionInterval(uint256)":{"params":{"_submissionInterval":"The new submission interval."}},"updateVerifier(address)":{"params":{"_verifier":"The new verifier address."}}},"stateVariables":{"aggregationVkey":{"custom:deprecated":""},"challenger":{"custom:network-specific":""},"fallbackTimeout":{"custom:network-specific":""},"finalizationPeriodSeconds":{"custom:network-specific":""},"l2BlockTime":{"custom:network-specific":""},"proposer":{"custom:network-specific":"@custom:deprecated"},"rangeVkeyCommitment":{"custom:deprecated":""},"rollupConfigHash":{"custom:deprecated":""},"submissionInterval":{"custom:network-specific":""},"version":{"custom:semver":"v3.0.0"}},"version":1},"userdoc":{"errors":{"L1BlockHashNotAvailable()":[{"notice":"The L1 block hash is not available. If the block hash requested is not in the last 256 blocks,         it is not available."}],"L1BlockHashNotCheckpointed()":[{"notice":"The L1 block hash is not checkpointed."}]},"events":{"DisputeGameFactorySet(address)":{"notice":"Emitted when the dispute game factory address is set."},"OpSuccinctConfigDeleted(bytes32)":{"notice":"Emitted when an OP Succinct configuration is deleted."},"OpSuccinctConfigUpdated(bytes32,bytes32,bytes32,bytes32)":{"notice":"Emitted when an OP Succinct configuration is updated."},"OptimisticModeToggled(bool,uint256)":{"notice":"Emitted when the optimistic mode is toggled."},"OutputProposed(bytes32,uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Emitted when an output is proposed."},"OutputsDeleted(uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Emitted when outputs are deleted."},"OwnershipTransferred(address,address)":{"notice":"Emitted when the owner address is updated."},"ProposerUpdated(address,bool)":{"notice":"Emitted when a proposer address is added."},"SubmissionIntervalUpdated(uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Emitted when the submission interval is updated."},"VerifierUpdated(address,address)":{"notice":"Emitted when the verifier address is updated."}},"kind":"user","methods":{"GENESIS_CONFIG_NAME()":{"notice":"The genesis configuration name."},"addOpSuccinctConfig(bytes32,bytes32,bytes32,bytes32)":{"notice":"Updates or creates an OP Succinct configuration."},"addProposer(address)":{"notice":"Adds a new proposer address."},"aggregationVkey()":{"notice":"The verification key of the aggregation SP1 program."},"approvedProposers(address)":{"notice":"The proposers that can propose new proofs."},"challenger()":{"notice":"The address of the challenger. Can be updated via upgrade."},"checkpointBlockHash(uint256)":{"notice":"Checkpoints a block hash at a given block number."},"computeL2Timestamp(uint256)":{"notice":"Returns the L2 timestamp corresponding to a given L2 block number."},"constructor":{"notice":"Constructs the OPSuccinctL2OutputOracle contract. Disables initializers."},"deleteL2Outputs(uint256)":{"notice":"Deletes all output proposals after and including the proposal that corresponds to         the given output index. Only the challenger address can delete outputs.         The genesis output (index 0) cannot be deleted."},"deleteOpSuccinctConfig(bytes32)":{"notice":"Deletes an OP Succinct configuration."},"dgfProposeL2Output(bytes32,bytes32,uint256,uint256,bytes,address)":{"notice":"Proposes an L2 output through the dispute game factory."},"disableOptimisticMode(uint256)":{"notice":"Disables optimistic mode."},"disputeGameFactory()":{"notice":"The dispute game factory contract. If running with the L2OutputOracle directly, without the OPSuccinctDisputeGame wrapper, this is set to the zero address."},"enableOptimisticMode(uint256)":{"notice":"Enables optimistic mode."},"fallbackTimeout()":{"notice":"The time threshold (in seconds) after which anyone can submit a proposal if no proposal has been submitted.         Only applies in permissioned mode."},"finalizationPeriodSeconds()":{"notice":"The minimum time (in seconds) that must elapse before a withdrawal can be finalized."},"getL2Output(uint256)":{"notice":"Returns an output by index. Needed to return a struct instead of a tuple."},"getL2OutputAfter(uint256)":{"notice":"Returns the L2 output proposal that checkpoints a given L2 block number.         Uses a binary search to find the first output greater than or equal to the given         block."},"getL2OutputIndexAfter(uint256)":{"notice":"Returns the index of the L2 output that checkpoints a given L2 block number.         Uses a binary search to find the first output greater than or equal to the given         block."},"historicBlockHashes(uint256)":{"notice":"A trusted mapping of block numbers to block hashes."},"initialize((address,address,address,uint256,uint256,bytes32,bytes32,bytes32,bytes32,uint256,uint256,uint256,address,uint256))":{"notice":"Initializer."},"initializerVersion()":{"notice":"The version of the initializer on the contract. Used for managing upgrades."},"isValidOpSuccinctConfig((bytes32,bytes32,bytes32))":{"notice":"Validates that an OpSuccinctConfig has all non-zero parameters."},"l2BlockTime()":{"notice":"The time between L2 blocks in seconds. Once set, this value MUST NOT be modified."},"lastProposalTimestamp()":{"notice":"Returns the timestamp of the last submitted L2 output proposal.         If no proposals have been submitted yet, returns the starting timestamp."},"latestBlockNumber()":{"notice":"Returns the block number of the latest submitted L2 output proposal.         If no proposals been submitted yet then this function will return the starting         block number."},"latestOutputIndex()":{"notice":"Returns the number of outputs that have been proposed.         Will revert if no outputs have been proposed yet."},"nextBlockNumber()":{"notice":"Computes the block number of the next L2 block that needs to be checkpointed."},"nextOutputIndex()":{"notice":"Returns the index of the next output to be proposed."},"opSuccinctConfigs(bytes32)":{"notice":"Mapping of configuration names to OpSuccinctConfig structs."},"optimisticMode()":{"notice":"Activate optimistic mode. When true, the contract will accept outputs without verification."},"owner()":{"notice":"The owner of the contract, who has admin permissions."},"proposeL2Output(bytes32,bytes32,uint256,uint256,bytes,address)":{"notice":"Accepts an outputRoot and the timestamp of the corresponding L2 block.         The timestamp must be equal to the current value returned by `nextTimestamp()` in         order to be accepted. This function may only be called by the Proposer."},"proposeL2Output(bytes32,uint256,bytes32,uint256)":{"notice":"Accepts an outputRoot and the timestamp of the corresponding L2 block.         The timestamp must be equal to the current value returned by `nextTimestamp()` in         order to be accepted. This function may only be called by the Proposer."},"proposer()":{"notice":"The address of the proposer. Can be updated via upgrade. DEPRECATED: Use approvedProposers mapping instead."},"rangeVkeyCommitment()":{"notice":"The 32 byte commitment to the BabyBear representation of the verification key of the range SP1 program. Specifically, this verification is the output of converting the [u32; 8] range BabyBear verification key to a [u8; 32] array."},"removeProposer(address)":{"notice":"Removes a proposer address."},"rollupConfigHash()":{"notice":"The hash of the chain's rollup config, which ensures the proofs submitted are for the correct chain."},"setDisputeGameFactory(address)":{"notice":"Sets the dispute game factory address."},"startingBlockNumber()":{"notice":"The number of the first L2 block recorded in this contract."},"startingTimestamp()":{"notice":"The timestamp of the first L2 block recorded in this contract."},"submissionInterval()":{"notice":"The minimum interval in L2 blocks at which checkpoints must be submitted."},"transferOwnership(address)":{"notice":"Updates the owner address."},"updateSubmissionInterval(uint256)":{"notice":"Update the submission interval."},"updateVerifier(address)":{"notice":"Updates the verifier address."},"verifier()":{"notice":"The deployed SP1Verifier contract to verify proofs."},"version()":{"notice":"Semantic version."}},"notice":"The OPSuccinctL2OutputOracle contains an array of L2 state outputs, where each output is a         commitment to the state of the L2 chain. Other contracts like the OptimismPortal use         these outputs to verify information about the state of L2. The outputs posted to this contract         are proved to be valid with checkpointBlockHash(uint256).details: If the block hash is not available, this will revert.

params._blockNumber: Block number to checkpoint the hash at.

params._l2BlockNumber: The L2 block number of the target block.

returns._0: L2 timestamp of the given block.

params._l2OutputIndex: Index of the first L2 output to be deleted. Must be greater than 0.                       All outputs after this output will also be deleted.

params._configName: The name of the configuration to delete.

dgfProposeL2Output(bytes32,bytes32,uint2.details: We can only invoke `disputeGameFactory.create` through this function, ensuring that      no one can interact with `proposeL2Output` directly when the dispute game factory is set.

params._configName: The name of the OpSuccinctConfig to use.
params._l1BlockNumber: The L1 block number of the output to propose.
params._l2BlockNumber: The L2 block number of the output to propose.
params._outputRoot: The root of the L2 output to propose.
params._proof: The proof of the output to propose.
params._proverAddress: The address of the prover to use.

params._l2OutputIndex: Index of the output to return.

returns._0: The output at the given index.

params._l2BlockNumber: L2 block number to find a checkpoint for.

returns._0: First checkpoint that commits to the given L2 block number.

params._l2BlockNumber: L2 block number to find a checkpoint for.

returns._0: Index of the first checkpoint that commits to the given L2 block number.

params._initParams: The initialization parameters for the contract.

returns._0: True if all parameters are non-zero, false otherwise.
Timestamp of the latest submitted L2 output proposal.
The number of outputs that have been proposed.
The index of the next output to be proposed.

proposeL2Output(bytes32,bytes32,uint256,.details: Modified the function signature to exclude the `_l1BlockHash` parameter, as it's redundant      for OP Succinct given the `_l1BlockNumber` parameter.Security Note: This contract uses `tx.origin` for proposer permission control due to usage of this contract      in the OPSuccinctDisputeGame, created via DisputeGameFactory using the Clone With Immutable Arguments (CWIA) pattern.      In this setup:      - `msg.sender` is the newly created game contract, not an approved proposer.      - `tx.origin` identifies the actual user initiating the transaction.      While `tx.origin` can be vulnerable in general, it is safe here because:      - Only trusted proposers/relayers call this contract.      - Proposers are expected to interact solely with trusted contracts.      As long as proposers avoid untrusted contracts, `tx.origin` is as secure as `msg.sender` in this context.

params._configName: The name of the OP Succinct configuration to use.
params._l1BlockNumber: The block number with the specified block hash.
params._l2BlockNumber: The L2 block number that resulted in _outputRoot.
params._outputRoot: The L2 output of the checkpoint block.
params._proof: The aggregation proof that proves the transition from the latest L2 output to the new L2 output.
params._proverAddress: The address of the prover that submitted the proof. Note: proverAddress is not required to be the tx.origin as there is no reason to front-run the prover in the full validity setting.

proposeL2Output(bytes32,uint256,bytes32,.details: This function is sourced from the original L2OutputOracle contract. The only modification is that the proposer address must be in the approvedProposers mapping, or permissionless proposing is enabled.This function is not compatible with the `OPSuccinctDisputeGame` contract as it uses `msg.sender` for proposer permission control.      See `whenNotOptimistic` implementation of `proposeL2Output` for more details.      If the functionality for optimistic mode is needed in the `OPSuccinctDisputeGame` contract, use mock mode instead.

params._l1BlockHash: A block hash which must be included in the current chain.
params._l1BlockNumber: The block number with the specified block hash.
params._l2BlockNumber: The L2 block number that resulted in _outputRoot.
params._outputRoot: The L2 output of the checkpoint block.

addOpSuccinctConfig(bytes32,bytes32,byte.notice: Updates or creates an OP Succinct configuration.

aggregationVkey().notice: The verification key of the aggregation SP1 program.

approvedProposers(address).notice: The proposers that can propose new proofs.

challenger().notice: The address of the challenger. Can be updated via upgrade.

checkpointBlockHash(uint256).notice: Checkpoints a block hash at a given block number.

computeL2Timestamp(uint256).notice: Returns the L2 timestamp corresponding to a given L2 block number.

constructor.notice: Constructs the OPSuccinctL2OutputOracle contract. Disables initializers.

deleteL2Outputs(uint256).notice: Deletes all output proposals after and including the proposal that corresponds to         the given output index. Only the challenger address can delete outputs.         The genesis output (index 0) cannot be deleted.

dgfProposeL2Output(bytes32,bytes32,uint2.notice: Proposes an L2 output through the dispute game factory.

disputeGameFactory().notice: The dispute game factory contract. If running with the L2OutputOracle directly, without the OPSuccinctDisputeGame wrapper, this is set to the zero address.

fallbackTimeout().notice: The time threshold (in seconds) after which anyone can submit a proposal if no proposal has been submitted.         Only applies in permissioned mode.

finalizationPeriodSeconds().notice: The minimum time (in seconds) that must elapse before a withdrawal can be finalized.

getL2Output(uint256).notice: Returns an output by index. Needed to return a struct instead of a tuple.

getL2OutputAfter(uint256).notice: Returns the L2 output proposal that checkpoints a given L2 block number.         Uses a binary search to find the first output greater than or equal to the given         block.

getL2OutputIndexAfter(uint256).notice: Returns the index of the L2 output that checkpoints a given L2 block number.         Uses a binary search to find the first output greater than or equal to the given         block.

historicBlockHashes(uint256).notice: A trusted mapping of block numbers to block hashes.

initializerVersion().notice: The version of the initializer on the contract. Used for managing upgrades.

isValidOpSuccinctConfig((bytes32,bytes32.notice: Validates that an OpSuccinctConfig has all non-zero parameters.

l2BlockTime().notice: The time between L2 blocks in seconds. Once set, this value MUST NOT be modified.

lastProposalTimestamp().notice: Returns the timestamp of the last submitted L2 output proposal.         If no proposals have been submitted yet, returns the starting timestamp.

latestBlockNumber().notice: Returns the block number of the latest submitted L2 output proposal.         If no proposals been submitted yet then this function will return the starting         block number.

latestOutputIndex().notice: Returns the number of outputs that have been proposed.         Will revert if no outputs have been proposed yet.

nextBlockNumber().notice: Computes the block number of the next L2 block that needs to be checkpointed.

nextOutputIndex().notice: Returns the index of the next output to be proposed.

opSuccinctConfigs(bytes32).notice: Mapping of configuration names to OpSuccinctConfig structs.

optimisticMode().notice: Activate optimistic mode. When true, the contract will accept outputs without verification.

owner().notice: The owner of the contract, who has admin permissions.

proposeL2Output(bytes32,bytes32,uint256,.notice: Accepts an outputRoot and the timestamp of the corresponding L2 block.         The timestamp must be equal to the current value returned by `nextTimestamp()` in         order to be accepted. This function may only be called by the Proposer.

proposeL2Output(bytes32,uint256,bytes32,.notice: Accepts an outputRoot and the timestamp of the corresponding L2 block.         The timestamp must be equal to the current value returned by `nextTimestamp()` in         order to be accepted. This function may only be called by the Proposer.

proposer().notice: The address of the proposer. Can be updated via upgrade. DEPRECATED: Use approvedProposers mapping instead.

rangeVkeyCommitment().notice: The 32 byte commitment to the BabyBear representation of the verification key of the range SP1 program. Specifically, this verification is the output of converting the [u32; 8] range BabyBear verification key to a [u8; 32] array.

rollupConfigHash().notice: The hash of the chain's rollup config, which ensures the proofs submitted are for the correct chain.

setDisputeGameFactory(address).notice: Sets the dispute game factory address.

startingBlockNumber().notice: The number of the first L2 block recorded in this contract.

startingTimestamp().notice: The timestamp of the first L2 block recorded in this contract.

submissionInterval().notice: The minimum interval in L2 blocks at which checkpoints must be submitted.

verifier().notice: The deployed SP1Verifier contract to verify proofs.
